<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:温泉法]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11562953]] Wikidata name: Hot Springs Act [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Laws and Regulations of Japan |題名=温泉法 |提出区分=閣法 |効力=現行法 |種類=[[:en:Environmental law|Environmental law]] |内容=温泉の保護、採取に伴う災害の防止および利用 |関連={{ill|Nature Conservation Law|ja|自然環境保全法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nature Conservation Law|1=Nature Conservation Act|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612867}} |リンク={{Egov law}} |ウィキソース=温泉法 }}
'''Onsen Law''' (温泉法, 昭和23rd year 法律第125号) is a law in Japan concerning the protection of [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]].
== Purpose == This law aims to protect hot springs, prevent disasters caused by the extraction of combustible [[:en:Natural gas|natural gas]], and ensure the proper utilization of hot springs, thereby contributing to the enhancement of [[:en:General welfare clause|public welfare]] (Article 1).
== Content == The law stipulates regulations for land excavation to extract hot springs, permissions for hot spring extraction, appropriate utilization, and display of {{ill|hot spring analysis report|ja|温泉分析書|1=Hot spring analysis report|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hot spring analysis report|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot spring analysis report|qq=}} details.
Recent amendments address the {{ill|Onsen fraud issue|ja|温泉偽装問題|1=Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=Onsen Fraud Scandal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen fraud issue|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=}} by mandating hot spring operators to submit an updated hot spring analysis report every 10 years as prescribed by the Onsen Law Enforcement Order. Following the {{ill|Shibuya Onsen Facility Explosion Incident|ja|渋谷温泉施設爆発事故|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Onsen Facility Explosion Incident|1=Shibuya Onsen Facility Explosion Incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561755}}, stricter guidelines were introduced for land excavation permissions and hot spring extraction permits to prevent disasters caused by combustible natural gas.
== Definition of Onsen == According to Article 2, Paragraph 1, onsen refers to hot water, mineral water, or gases (excluding natural gases primarily composed of hydrocarbons) that meet one of the following criteria:
# Water temperature at the source is 25°C or higher. (Water below 25°C may be referred to as [[:en:Reizei|cold springs]] or [[:en:Mineral spring|Mineral spring]].) # Contains at least one of the following substances in the specified minimum quantities per kilogram: {|class=wikitable style="font-size:small" ! Substance !! Minimum content per kg |- | Dissolved solids (excluding gases) || style="text-align:right"| 1,000 mg |- | Free [[:en:Carbonic acid|carbon dioxide]] (CO<sub>2</sub>) || style="text-align:right"| 250 mg |- | [[:en:Lithium|Lithium ion]] (Li<sup>+</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 1 mg |- | [[:en:Strontium|Strontium ion]] (Sr<sup>++</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 10 mg |- | [[:en:Barium|Barium ion]] (Ba<sup>++</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 5 mg |- | [[:en:Iron|Iron ions]] (Fe<sup>++</sup>, Fe<sup>+++</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 10 mg |- | Manganese ion (Mn<sup>++</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 10 mg |- | [[:en:Hydrogen|Hydrogen ion]] (H<sup>+</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 1 mg |- | [[:en:Bromine|Bromine ion]] (Br<sup>-</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 5 mg |- | {{ill|Iodine ion|ja|沃素|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iodine ion|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヨウ素|1=iodine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1103}} (I<sup>-</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 1 mg |- | [[:en:Fluorine|Fluoride ion]] (F<sup>-</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 2 mg |- | Hydrogen arsenate ion (HAsO<sub>4</sub><sup>--</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 1.3 mg |- | Metarsenous acid (HAsO<sub>2</sub>) || style="text-align:right"| 1 mg |- | Total [[:en:Sulfur|sulfur]] (S) [HS<sup>-</sup>, S<sub>2</sub>O<sub>3</sub><sup>--</sup>, H<sub>2</sub>S] || style="text-align:right"| 1 mg |- | [[:en:Boric acid|Metaboric acid]] (HBO<sub>2</sub>) || style="text-align:right"| 5 mg |- | [[:en:Silicic acid|Metasilicic acid]] (H<sub>2</sub>SiO<sub>3</sub>) || style="text-align:right"| 50 mg |- | Sodium bicarbonate (NaHCO<sub>3</sub>) || style="text-align:right"| 340 mg |- | [[:en:Radon|Radon]] (Rn) || style="text-align:right"| 20×10<sup>-10</sup> Ci |- | [[:en:Radium|Radium salts]] (as Ra) || style="text-align:right"| 1×10<sup>-8</sup> mg |}
== Structure == * Chapter 1: General Provisions (Articles 1-2) * Chapter 2: Protection of Onsen (Articles 3-14) * Chapter 3: Prevention of Disasters Related to Onsen Extraction (Articles 14-2 to 14-10) * Chapter 4: Utilization of Onsen (Articles 15-31) * Chapter 5: Advisory and Hearings (Articles 32-33) * Chapter 6: Miscellaneous Provisions (Articles 34-37) * Chapter 7: Penal Provisions (Articles 38-43) * Supplementary Provisions == See Also == * {{ill|Hot spring analysis report|ja|温泉分析書|1=Hot spring analysis report|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot spring analysis report|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot spring analysis report|qq=}} * {{ill|Mineral Spring Analysis Guidelines|ja|鉱泉分析法指針|1=Guidelines for Mineral Spring Analysis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mineral Spring Analysis Guidelines|13=User:Immanuelle/Guidelines for Mineral Spring Analysis|qq=}} * {{ill|Onsen fraud issue|ja|温泉偽装問題|1=Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=Onsen Fraud Scandal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen fraud issue|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=}} * {{ill|Bathing tax|ja|入湯税|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bathing tax|1=Bath tax|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388992}}
== External Links == * [https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/index.html Protection and Utilization of Onsen] - Ministry of the Environment * [https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/pamph_aramashi.html Overview of Onsen Law Revisions] - Ministry of the Environment * [https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=323AC0000000125&openerCode=1 Onsen Law] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/e-Gov Law Search|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:温泉法]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11562953]] Wikidata name: Hot Springs Act [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Laws and Regulations of Japan |題名=温泉法 |提出区分=閣法 |効力=現行法 |種類=[[:en:Environmental law|Environmental law]] |内容=温泉の保護、採取に伴う災害の防止および利用 |関連={{ill|Nature Conservation Law|ja|自然環境保全法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nature Conservation Law|1=Nature Conservation Act|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612867}} |リンク={{Egov law}} |ウィキソース=温泉法 }}
'''Onsen Law''' (温泉法, 昭和23rd year 法律第125号) is a law in Japan concerning the protection of [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]].
== Purpose == This law aims to protect hot springs, prevent disasters caused by the extraction of combustible [[:en:Natural gas|natural gas]], and ensure the proper utilization of hot springs, thereby contributing to the enhancement of [[:en:General welfare clause|public welfare]] (Article 1).
== Content == The law stipulates regulations for land excavation to extract hot springs, permissions for hot spring extraction, appropriate utilization, and display of {{ill|hot spring analysis report|ja|温泉分析書|1=Hot spring analysis report|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hot spring analysis report|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot spring analysis report|qq=}} details.
Recent amendments address the {{ill|Onsen fraud issue|ja|温泉偽装問題|1=Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=Onsen Fraud Scandal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen fraud issue|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=}} by mandating hot spring operators to submit an updated hot spring analysis report every 10 years as prescribed by the Onsen Law Enforcement Order. Following the {{ill|Shibuya Onsen Facility Explosion Incident|ja|渋谷温泉施設爆発事故|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Onsen Facility Explosion Incident|1=Shibuya Onsen Facility Explosion Incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561755}}, stricter guidelines were introduced for land excavation permissions and hot spring extraction permits to prevent disasters caused by combustible natural gas.
== Definition of Onsen == According to Article 2, Paragraph 1, onsen refers to hot water, mineral water, or gases (excluding natural gases primarily composed of hydrocarbons) that meet one of the following criteria:
# Water temperature at the source is 25°C or higher. (Water below 25°C may be referred to as [[:en:Reizei|cold springs]] or [[:en:Mineral spring|Mineral spring]].) # Contains at least one of the following substances in the specified minimum quantities per kilogram: {|class=wikitable style="font-size:small" ! Substance !! Minimum content per kg |- | Dissolved solids (excluding gases) || style="text-align:right"| 1,000 mg |- | Free [[:en:Carbonic acid|carbon dioxide]] (CO<sub>2</sub>) || style="text-align:right"| 250 mg |- | [[:en:Lithium|Lithium ion]] (Li<sup>+</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 1 mg |- | [[:en:Strontium|Strontium ion]] (Sr<sup>++</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 10 mg |- | [[:en:Barium|Barium ion]] (Ba<sup>++</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 5 mg |- | [[:en:Iron|Iron ions]] (Fe<sup>++</sup>, Fe<sup>+++</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 10 mg |- | Manganese ion (Mn<sup>++</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 10 mg |- | [[:en:Hydrogen|Hydrogen ion]] (H<sup>+</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 1 mg |- | [[:en:Bromine|Bromine ion]] (Br<sup>-</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 5 mg |- | {{ill|Iodine ion|ja|沃素|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iodine ion|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヨウ素|1=iodine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1103}} (I<sup>-</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 1 mg |- | [[:en:Fluorine|Fluoride ion]] (F<sup>-</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 2 mg |- | Hydrogen arsenate ion (HAsO<sub>4</sub><sup>--</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 1.3 mg |- | Metarsenous acid (HAsO<sub>2</sub>) || style="text-align:right"| 1 mg |- | Total [[:en:Sulfur|sulfur]] (S) [HS<sup>-</sup>, S<sub>2</sub>O<sub>3</sub><sup>--</sup>, H<sub>2</sub>S] || style="text-align:right"| 1 mg |- | [[:en:Boric acid|Metaboric acid]] (HBO<sub>2</sub>) || style="text-align:right"| 5 mg |- | [[:en:Silicic acid|Metasilicic acid]] (H<sub>2</sub>SiO<sub>3</sub>) || style="text-align:right"| 50 mg |- | Sodium bicarbonate (NaHCO<sub>3</sub>) || style="text-align:right"| 340 mg |- | [[:en:Radon|Radon]] (Rn) || style="text-align:right"| 20×10<sup>-10</sup> Ci |- | [[:en:Radium|Radium salts]] (as Ra) || style="text-align:right"| 1×10<sup>-8</sup> mg |}
== Structure == * Chapter 1: General Provisions (Articles 1-2) * Chapter 2: Protection of Onsen (Articles 3-14) * Chapter 3: Prevention of Disasters Related to Onsen Extraction (Articles 14-2 to 14-10) * Chapter 4: Utilization of Onsen (Articles 15-31) * Chapter 5: Advisory and Hearings (Articles 32-33) * Chapter 6: Miscellaneous Provisions (Articles 34-37) * Chapter 7: Penal Provisions (Articles 38-43) * Supplementary Provisions == See Also == * {{ill|Hot spring analysis report|ja|温泉分析書|1=Hot spring analysis report|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot spring analysis report|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot spring analysis report|qq=}} * {{ill|Mineral Spring Analysis Guidelines|ja|鉱泉分析法指針|1=Guidelines for Mineral Spring Analysis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mineral Spring Analysis Guidelines|13=User:Immanuelle/Guidelines for Mineral Spring Analysis|qq=}} * {{ill|Onsen fraud issue|ja|温泉偽装問題|1=Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=Onsen Fraud Scandal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen fraud issue|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=}} * {{ill|Bathing tax|ja|入湯税|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bathing tax|1=Bath tax|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388992}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:温泉法]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:温泉法]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:温泉法]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11562953]] Wikidata name: Hot Springs Act [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Laws and Regulations of Japan |題名=温泉法 |提出区分=閣法 |効力=現行法 |種類=[[:en:Environmental law|Environmental law]] |内容=温泉の保護、採取に伴う災害の防止および利用 |関連={{ill|Nature Conservation Law|ja|自然環境保全法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nature Conservation Law|1=Nature Conservation Act|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612867}} |リンク={{Egov law}} |ウィキソース=温泉法 }}
'''Onsen Law''' (温泉法, 昭和23rd year 法律第125号) is a law in Japan concerning the protection of [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]].
== Purpose == This law aims to protect hot springs, prevent disasters caused by the extraction of combustible [[:en:Natural gas|natural gas]], and ensure the proper utilization of hot springs, thereby contributing to the enhancement of [[:en:General welfare clause|public welfare]] (Article 1).
== Content == The law stipulates regulations for land excavation to extract hot springs, permissions for hot spring extraction, appropriate utilization, and display of {{ill|hot spring analysis report|ja|温泉分析書|1=Hot spring analysis report|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hot spring analysis report|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot spring analysis report|qq=}} details.
Recent amendments address the {{ill|Onsen fraud issue|ja|温泉偽装問題|1=Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=Onsen Fraud Scandal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen fraud issue|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=}} by mandating hot spring operators to submit an updated hot spring analysis report every 10 years as prescribed by the Onsen Law Enforcement Order. Following the {{ill|Shibuya Onsen Facility Explosion Incident|ja|渋谷温泉施設爆発事故|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Onsen Facility Explosion Incident|1=Shibuya Onsen Facility Explosion Incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561755}}, stricter guidelines were introduced for land excavation permissions and hot spring extraction permits to prevent disasters caused by combustible natural gas.
== Definition of Onsen == According to Article 2, Paragraph 1, onsen refers to hot water, mineral water, or gases (excluding natural gases primarily composed of hydrocarbons) that meet one of the following criteria:
# Water temperature at the source is 25°C or higher. (Water below 25°C may be referred to as [[:en:Reizei|cold springs]] or [[:en:Mineral spring|Mineral spring]].) # Contains at least one of the following substances in the specified minimum quantities per kilogram: {|class=wikitable style="font-size:small" ! Substance !! Minimum content per kg |- | Dissolved solids (excluding gases) || style="text-align:right"| 1,000 mg |- | Free [[:en:Carbonic acid|carbon dioxide]] (CO<sub>2</sub>) || style="text-align:right"| 250 mg |- | [[:en:Lithium|Lithium ion]] (Li<sup>+</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 1 mg |- | [[:en:Strontium|Strontium ion]] (Sr<sup>++</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 10 mg |- | [[:en:Barium|Barium ion]] (Ba<sup>++</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 5 mg |- | [[:en:Iron|Iron ions]] (Fe<sup>++</sup>, Fe<sup>+++</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 10 mg |- | Manganese ion (Mn<sup>++</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 10 mg |- | [[:en:Hydrogen|Hydrogen ion]] (H<sup>+</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 1 mg |- | [[:en:Bromine|Bromine ion]] (Br<sup>-</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 5 mg |- | {{ill|Iodine ion|ja|沃素|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iodine ion|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヨウ素|1=iodine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1103}} (I<sup>-</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 1 mg |- | [[:en:Fluorine|Fluoride ion]] (F<sup>-</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 2 mg |- | Hydrogen arsenate ion (HAsO<sub>4</sub><sup>--</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 1.3 mg |- | Metarsenous acid (HAsO<sub>2</sub>) || style="text-align:right"| 1 mg |- | Total [[:en:Sulfur|sulfur]] (S) [HS<sup>-</sup>, S<sub>2</sub>O<sub>3</sub><sup>--</sup>, H<sub>2</sub>S] || style="text-align:right"| 1 mg |- | [[:en:Boric acid|Metaboric acid]] (HBO<sub>2</sub>) || style="text-align:right"| 5 mg |- | [[:en:Silicic acid|Metasilicic acid]] (H<sub>2</sub>SiO<sub>3</sub>) || style="text-align:right"| 50 mg |- | Sodium bicarbonate (NaHCO<sub>3</sub>) || style="text-align:right"| 340 mg |- | [[:en:Radon|Radon]] (Rn) || style="text-align:right"| 20×10<sup>-10</sup> Ci |- | [[:en:Radium|Radium salts]] (as Ra) || style="text-align:right"| 1×10<sup>-8</sup> mg |}
== Structure == * Chapter 1: General Provisions (Articles 1-2) * Chapter 2: Protection of Onsen (Articles 3-14) * Chapter 3: Prevention of Disasters Related to Onsen Extraction (Articles 14-2 to 14-10) * Chapter 4: Utilization of Onsen (Articles 15-31) * Chapter 5: Advisory and Hearings (Articles 32-33) * Chapter 6: Miscellaneous Provisions (Articles 34-37) * Chapter 7: Penal Provisions (Articles 38-43) * Supplementary Provisions == See Also == * {{ill|Hot spring analysis report|ja|温泉分析書|1=Hot spring analysis report|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot spring analysis report|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot spring analysis report|qq=}} * {{ill|Mineral Spring Analysis Guidelines|ja|鉱泉分析法指針|1=Guidelines for Mineral Spring Analysis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mineral Spring Analysis Guidelines|13=User:Immanuelle/Guidelines for Mineral Spring Analysis|qq=}} * {{ill|Onsen fraud issue|ja|温泉偽装問題|1=Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=Onsen Fraud Scandal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen fraud issue|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=}} * {{ill|Bathing tax|ja|入湯税|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bathing tax|1=Bath tax|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388992}}
== External Links == * [https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/index.html Protection and Utilization of Onsen] - Ministry of the Environment * [https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/pamph_aramashi.html Overview of Onsen Law Revisions] - Ministry of the Environment * [https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=323AC0000000125&openerCode=1 Onsen Law] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/e-Gov Law Search|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:温泉法]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:温泉法]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:温泉法]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11562953]] Wikidata name: Hot Springs Act [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Laws and Regulations of Japan |題名=温泉法 |提出区分=閣法 |効力=現行法 |種類=[[:en:Environmental law|Environmental law]] |内容=温泉の保護、採取に伴う災害の防止および利用 |関連={{ill|Nature Conservation Law|ja|自然環境保全法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nature Conservation Law|1=Nature Conservation Act|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612867}} |リンク={{Egov law}} |ウィキソース=温泉法 }}
'''Onsen Law''' (温泉法, 昭和23rd year 法律第125号) is a law in Japan concerning the protection of [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]].
== Purpose == This law aims to protect hot springs, prevent disasters caused by the extraction of combustible [[:en:Natural gas|natural gas]], and ensure the proper utilization of hot springs, thereby contributing to the enhancement of [[:en:General welfare clause|public welfare]] (Article 1).
== Content == The law stipulates regulations for land excavation to extract hot springs, permissions for hot spring extraction, appropriate utilization, and display of {{ill|hot spring analysis report|ja|温泉分析書|1=Hot spring analysis report|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hot spring analysis report|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot spring analysis report|qq=}} details.
Recent amendments address the {{ill|Onsen fraud issue|ja|温泉偽装問題|1=Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=Onsen Fraud Scandal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen fraud issue|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=}} by mandating hot spring operators to submit an updated hot spring analysis report every 10 years as prescribed by the Onsen Law Enforcement Order. Following the {{ill|Shibuya Onsen Facility Explosion Incident|ja|渋谷温泉施設爆発事故|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Onsen Facility Explosion Incident|1=Shibuya Onsen Facility Explosion Incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561755}}, stricter guidelines were introduced for land excavation permissions and hot spring extraction permits to prevent disasters caused by combustible natural gas.
== Definition of Onsen == According to Article 2, Paragraph 1, onsen refers to hot water, mineral water, or gases (excluding natural gases primarily composed of hydrocarbons) that meet one of the following criteria:
# Water temperature at the source is 25°C or higher. (Water below 25°C may be referred to as [[:en:Reizei|cold springs]] or [[:en:Mineral spring|Mineral spring]].) # Contains at least one of the following substances in the specified minimum quantities per kilogram: {|class=wikitable style="font-size:small" ! Substance !! Minimum content per kg |- | Dissolved solids (excluding gases) || style="text-align:right"| 1,000 mg |- | Free [[:en:Carbonic acid|carbon dioxide]] (CO<sub>2</sub>) || style="text-align:right"| 250 mg |- | [[:en:Lithium|Lithium ion]] (Li<sup>+</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 1 mg |- | [[:en:Strontium|Strontium ion]] (Sr<sup>++</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 10 mg |- | [[:en:Barium|Barium ion]] (Ba<sup>++</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 5 mg |- | [[:en:Iron|Iron ions]] (Fe<sup>++</sup>, Fe<sup>+++</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 10 mg |- | Manganese ion (Mn<sup>++</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 10 mg |- | [[:en:Hydrogen|Hydrogen ion]] (H<sup>+</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 1 mg |- | [[:en:Bromine|Bromine ion]] (Br<sup>-</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 5 mg |- | {{ill|Iodine ion|ja|沃素|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iodine ion|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヨウ素|1=iodine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1103}} (I<sup>-</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 1 mg |- | [[:en:Fluorine|Fluoride ion]] (F<sup>-</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 2 mg |- | Hydrogen arsenate ion (HAsO<sub>4</sub><sup>--</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 1.3 mg |- | Metarsenous acid (HAsO<sub>2</sub>) || style="text-align:right"| 1 mg |- | Total [[:en:Sulfur|sulfur]] (S) [HS<sup>-</sup>, S<sub>2</sub>O<sub>3</sub><sup>--</sup>, H<sub>2</sub>S] || style="text-align:right"| 1 mg |- | [[:en:Boric acid|Metaboric acid]] (HBO<sub>2</sub>) || style="text-align:right"| 5 mg |- | [[:en:Silicic acid|Metasilicic acid]] (H<sub>2</sub>SiO<sub>3</sub>) || style="text-align:right"| 50 mg |- | Sodium bicarbonate (NaHCO<sub>3</sub>) || style="text-align:right"| 340 mg |- | [[:en:Radon|Radon]] (Rn) || style="text-align:right"| 20×10<sup>-10</sup> Ci |- | [[:en:Radium|Radium salts]] (as Ra) || style="text-align:right"| 1×10<sup>-8</sup> mg |}
== Structure == * Chapter 1: General Provisions (Articles 1-2) * Chapter 2: Protection of Onsen (Articles 3-14) * Chapter 3: Prevention of Disasters Related to Onsen Extraction (Articles 14-2 to 14-10) * Chapter 4: Utilization of Onsen (Articles 15-31) * Chapter 5: Advisory and Hearings (Articles 32-33) * Chapter 6: Miscellaneous Provisions (Articles 34-37) * Chapter 7: Penal Provisions (Articles 38-43) * Supplementary Provisions == See Also == * {{ill|Hot spring analysis report|ja|温泉分析書|1=Hot spring analysis report|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot spring analysis report|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot spring analysis report|qq=}} * {{ill|Mineral Spring Analysis Guidelines|ja|鉱泉分析法指針|1=Guidelines for Mineral Spring Analysis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mineral Spring Analysis Guidelines|13=User:Immanuelle/Guidelines for Mineral Spring Analysis|qq=}} * {{ill|Onsen fraud issue|ja|温泉偽装問題|1=Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=Onsen Fraud Scandal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen fraud issue|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=}} * {{ill|Bathing tax|ja|入湯税|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bathing tax|1=Bath tax|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388992}}

# Water temperature at the source is 25°C or higher. (Water below 25°C may be referred to as {{ill|Reizei|en|Reizei|WD=Q3423952|lt=cold springs}} or {{ill|Mineral spring|en|Mineral spring|WD=Q1365924|lt=Mineral spring}}.) # Contains at least one of the following substances in the specified minimum quantities per kilogram: {|class=wikitable style="font-size:small" ! <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:温泉法]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:温泉法]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:温泉法]]
{{Laws and Regulations of Japan |題名=温泉法 |提出区分=閣法 |効力=現行法 |種類={{ill|Environmental law|en|Environmental law|WD=Q328798|lt=Environmental law}} |内容=温泉の保護、採取に伴う災害の防止および利用 |関連={{ill|Nature Conservation Law|ja|自然環境保全法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nature Conservation Law|1=Nature Conservation Act|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612867}} |リンク={{Egov law}} |ウィキソース=温泉法 }}
'''Onsen Law''' (温泉法, 昭和23rd year 法律第125号) is a law in Japan concerning the protection of {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen}}.
== Purpose == This law aims to protect hot springs, prevent disasters caused by the extraction of combustible {{ill|Natural gas|en|Natural gas|WD=Q40858|lt=natural gas}}, and ensure the proper utilization of hot springs, thereby contributing to the enhancement of {{ill|General welfare clause|en|General welfare clause|WD=Q17157085|lt=public welfare}} (Article 1).
== Content == The law stipulates regulations for land excavation to extract hot springs, permissions for hot spring extraction, appropriate utilization, and display of {{ill|hot spring analysis report|ja|温泉分析書|1=Hot spring analysis report|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hot spring analysis report|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot spring analysis report|qq=}} details.
Recent amendments address the {{ill|Onsen fraud issue|ja|温泉偽装問題|1=Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=Onsen Fraud Scandal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen fraud issue|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=}} by mandating hot spring operators to submit an updated hot spring analysis report every 10 years as prescribed by the Onsen Law Enforcement Order. Following the {{ill|Shibuya Onsen Facility Explosion Incident|ja|渋谷温泉施設爆発事故|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Onsen Facility Explosion Incident|1=Shibuya Onsen Facility Explosion Incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561755}}, stricter guidelines were introduced for land excavation permissions and hot spring extraction permits to prevent disasters caused by combustible natural gas.
== Definition of Onsen == According to Article 2, Paragraph 1, onsen refers to hot water, mineral water, or gases (excluding natural gases primarily composed of hydrocarbons) that meet one of the following criteria:
# Water temperature at the source is 25°C or higher. (Water below 25°C may be referred to as {{ill|Reizei|en|Reizei|WD=Q3423952|lt=cold springs}} or {{ill|Mineral spring|en|Mineral spring|WD=Q1365924|lt=Mineral spring}}.) # Contains at least one of the following substances in the specified minimum quantities per kilogram: {|class=wikitable style="font-size:small" ! Substance !! Minimum content per kg |- | Dissolved solids (excluding gases) || style="text-align:right"| 1,000 mg |- | Free {{ill|Carbonic acid|en|Carbonic acid|WD=Q104334|lt=carbon dioxide}} (CO<sub>2</sub>) || style="text-align:right"| 250 mg |- | {{ill|Lithium|en|Lithium|WD=Q568|lt=Lithium ion}} (Li<sup>+</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 1 mg |- | {{ill|Strontium|en|Strontium|WD=Q938|lt=Strontium ion}} (Sr<sup>++</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 10 mg |- | {{ill|Barium|en|Barium|WD=Q1112|lt=Barium ion}} (Ba<sup>++</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 5 mg |- | {{ill|Iron|en|Iron|WD=Q677|lt=Iron ions}} (Fe<sup>++</sup>, Fe<sup>+++</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 10 mg |- | Manganese ion (Mn<sup>++</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 10 mg |- | {{ill|Hydrogen|en|Hydrogen|WD=Q556|lt=Hydrogen ion}} (H<sup>+</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 1 mg |- | {{ill|Bromine|en|Bromine|WD=Q879|lt=Bromine ion}} (Br<sup>-</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 5 mg |- | {{ill|Iodine ion|ja|沃素|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iodine ion|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヨウ素|1=iodine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1103}} (I<sup>-</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 1 mg |- | {{ill|Fluorine|en|Fluorine|WD=Q650|lt=Fluoride ion}} (F<sup>-</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 2 mg |- | Hydrogen arsenate ion (HAsO<sub>4</sub><sup>--</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 1.3 mg |- | Metarsenous acid (HAsO<sub>2</sub>) || style="text-align:right"| 1 mg |- | Total {{ill|Sulfur|en|Sulfur|WD=Q682|lt=sulfur}} (S) [HS<sup>-</sup>, S<sub>2</sub>O<sub>3</sub><sup>--</sup>, H<sub>2</sub>S] || style="text-align:right"| 1 mg |- | {{ill|Boric acid|en|Boric acid|WD=Q187045|lt=Metaboric acid}} (HBO<sub>2</sub>) || style="text-align:right"| 5 mg |- | {{ill|Silicic acid|en|Silicic acid|WD=Q12457481|lt=Metasilicic acid}} (H<sub>2</sub>SiO<sub>3</sub>) || style="text-align:right"| 50 mg |- | Sodium bicarbonate (NaHCO<sub>3</sub>) || style="text-align:right"| 340 mg |- | {{ill|Radon|en|Radon|WD=Q1133|lt=Radon}} (Rn) || style="text-align:right"| 20×10<sup>-10</sup> Ci |- | {{ill|Radium|en|Radium|WD=Q1128|lt=Radium salts}} (as Ra) || style="text-align:right"| 1×10<sup>-8</sup> mg |}
== Structure == * Chapter 1: General Provisions (Articles 1-2) * Chapter 2: Protection of Onsen (Articles 3-14) * Chapter 3: Prevention of Disasters Related to Onsen Extraction (Articles 14-2 to 14-10) * Chapter 4: Utilization of Onsen (Articles 15-31) * Chapter 5: Advisory and Hearings (Articles 32-33) * Chapter 6: Miscellaneous Provisions (Articles 34-37) * Chapter 7: Penal Provisions (Articles 38-43) * Supplementary Provisions == See Also == * {{ill|Hot spring analysis report|ja|温泉分析書|1=Hot spring analysis report|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot spring analysis report|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot spring analysis report|qq=}} * {{ill|Mineral Spring Analysis Guidelines|ja|鉱泉分析法指針|1=Guidelines for Mineral Spring Analysis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mineral Spring Analysis Guidelines|13=User:Immanuelle/Guidelines for Mineral Spring Analysis|qq=}} * {{ill|Onsen fraud issue|ja|温泉偽装問題|1=Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=Onsen Fraud Scandal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen fraud issue|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=}} * {{ill|Bathing tax|ja|入湯税|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bathing tax|1=Bath tax|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388992}}
== External Links == * [https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/index.html Protection and Utilization of Onsen] - Ministry of the Environment * [https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/pamph_aramashi.html Overview of Onsen Law Revisions] - Ministry of the Environment * [https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=323AC0000000125&openerCode=1 Onsen Law] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/e-Gov Law Search|qq=}}

(NaHCO<sub>3</sub>) || style="text-align:right"| 340 mg |- | {{ill|Radon|en|Radon|WD=Q1133|lt=Radon}} (Rn) || style="text-align:right"| 20×10<sup>-10</sup> Ci |- | <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:温泉法]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:温泉法]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:温泉法]]
{{Laws and Regulations of Japan |題名=温泉法 |提出区分=閣法 |効力=現行法 |種類={{ill|Environmental law|en|Environmental law|WD=Q328798|lt=Environmental law}} |内容=温泉の保護、採取に伴う災害の防止および利用 |関連={{ill|Nature Conservation Law|ja|自然環境保全法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nature Conservation Law|1=Nature Conservation Act|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612867}} |リンク={{Egov law}} |ウィキソース=温泉法 }}
'''Onsen Law''' (温泉法, 昭和23rd year 法律第125号) is a law in Japan concerning the protection of {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen}}.
== Purpose == This law aims to protect hot springs, prevent disasters caused by the extraction of combustible {{ill|Natural gas|en|Natural gas|WD=Q40858|lt=natural gas}}, and ensure the proper utilization of hot springs, thereby contributing to the enhancement of {{ill|General welfare clause|en|General welfare clause|WD=Q17157085|lt=public welfare}} (Article 1).
== Content == The law stipulates regulations for land excavation to extract hot springs, permissions for hot spring extraction, appropriate utilization, and display of {{ill|hot spring analysis report|ja|温泉分析書|1=Hot spring analysis report|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hot spring analysis report|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot spring analysis report|qq=}} details.
Recent amendments address the {{ill|Onsen fraud issue|ja|温泉偽装問題|1=Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=Onsen Fraud Scandal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen fraud issue|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=}} by mandating hot spring operators to submit an updated hot spring analysis report every 10 years as prescribed by the Onsen Law Enforcement Order. Following the {{ill|Shibuya Onsen Facility Explosion Incident|ja|渋谷温泉施設爆発事故|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Onsen Facility Explosion Incident|1=Shibuya Onsen Facility Explosion Incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561755}}, stricter guidelines were introduced for land excavation permissions and hot spring extraction permits to prevent disasters caused by combustible natural gas.
== Definition of Onsen == According to Article 2, Paragraph 1, onsen refers to hot water, mineral water, or gases (excluding natural gases primarily composed of hydrocarbons) that meet one of the following criteria:
# Water temperature at the source is 25°C or higher. (Water below 25°C may be referred to as {{ill|Reizei|en|Reizei|WD=Q3423952|lt=cold springs}} or {{ill|Mineral spring|en|Mineral spring|WD=Q1365924|lt=Mineral spring}}.) # Contains at least one of the following substances in the specified minimum quantities per kilogram: {|class=wikitable style="font-size:small" ! Substance !! Minimum content per kg |- | Dissolved solids (excluding gases) || style="text-align:right"| 1,000 mg |- | Free {{ill|Carbonic acid|en|Carbonic acid|WD=Q104334|lt=carbon dioxide}} (CO<sub>2</sub>) || style="text-align:right"| 250 mg |- | {{ill|Lithium|en|Lithium|WD=Q568|lt=Lithium ion}} (Li<sup>+</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 1 mg |- | {{ill|Strontium|en|Strontium|WD=Q938|lt=Strontium ion}} (Sr<sup>++</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 10 mg |- | {{ill|Barium|en|Barium|WD=Q1112|lt=Barium ion}} (Ba<sup>++</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 5 mg |- | {{ill|Iron|en|Iron|WD=Q677|lt=Iron ions}} (Fe<sup>++</sup>, Fe<sup>+++</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 10 mg |- | Manganese ion (Mn<sup>++</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 10 mg |- | {{ill|Hydrogen|en|Hydrogen|WD=Q556|lt=Hydrogen ion}} (H<sup>+</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 1 mg |- | {{ill|Bromine|en|Bromine|WD=Q879|lt=Bromine ion}} (Br<sup>-</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 5 mg |- | {{ill|Iodine ion|ja|沃素|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iodine ion|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヨウ素|1=iodine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1103}} (I<sup>-</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 1 mg |- | {{ill|Fluorine|en|Fluorine|WD=Q650|lt=Fluoride ion}} (F<sup>-</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 2 mg |- | Hydrogen arsenate ion (HAsO<sub>4</sub><sup>--</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 1.3 mg |- | Metarsenous acid (HAsO<sub>2</sub>) || style="text-align:right"| 1 mg |- | Total {{ill|Sulfur|en|Sulfur|WD=Q682|lt=sulfur}} (S) [HS<sup>-</sup>, S<sub>2</sub>O<sub>3</sub><sup>--</sup>, H<sub>2</sub>S] || style="text-align:right"| 1 mg |- | {{ill|Boric acid|en|Boric acid|WD=Q187045|lt=Metaboric acid}} (HBO<sub>2</sub>) || style="text-align:right"| 5 mg |- | {{ill|Silicic acid|en|Silicic acid|WD=Q12457481|lt=Metasilicic acid}} (H<sub>2</sub>SiO<sub>3</sub>) || style="text-align:right"| 50 mg |- | Sodium bicarbonate (NaHCO<sub>3</sub>) || style="text-align:right"| 340 mg |- | {{ill|Radon|en|Radon|WD=Q1133|lt=Radon}} (Rn) || style="text-align:right"| 20×10<sup>-10</sup> Ci |- | {{ill|Radium|en|Radium|WD=Q1128|lt=Radium salts}} (as Ra) || style="text-align:right"| 1×10<sup>-8</sup> mg |}
== Structure == * Chapter 1: General Provisions (Articles 1-2) * Chapter 2: Protection of Onsen (Articles 3-14) * Chapter 3: Prevention of Disasters Related to Onsen Extraction (Articles 14-2 to 14-10) * Chapter 4: Utilization of Onsen (Articles 15-31) * Chapter 5: Advisory and Hearings (Articles 32-33) * Chapter 6: Miscellaneous Provisions (Articles 34-37) * Chapter 7: Penal Provisions (Articles 38-43) * Supplementary Provisions == See Also == * {{ill|Hot spring analysis report|ja|温泉分析書|1=Hot spring analysis report|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot spring analysis report|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot spring analysis report|qq=}} * {{ill|Mineral Spring Analysis Guidelines|ja|鉱泉分析法指針|1=Guidelines for Mineral Spring Analysis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mineral Spring Analysis Guidelines|13=User:Immanuelle/Guidelines for Mineral Spring Analysis|qq=}} * {{ill|Onsen fraud issue|ja|温泉偽装問題|1=Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=Onsen Fraud Scandal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen fraud issue|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=}} * {{ill|Bathing tax|ja|入湯税|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bathing tax|1=Bath tax|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388992}}
== External Links == * [https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/index.html Protection and Utilization of Onsen] - Ministry of the Environment * [https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/pamph_aramashi.html Overview of Onsen Law Revisions] - Ministry of the Environment * [https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=323AC0000000125&openerCode=1 Onsen Law] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/e-Gov Law Search|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:温泉法]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:温泉法]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:温泉法]]
{{Laws and Regulations of Japan |題名=温泉法 |提出区分=閣法 |効力=現行法 |種類={{ill|Environmental law|en|Environmental law|WD=Q328798|lt=Environmental law|1=Environmental law}} |内容=温泉の保護、採取に伴う災害の防止および利用 |関連={{ill|Nature Conservation Law|ja|自然環境保全法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nature Conservation Law|1=Nature Conservation Act|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612867}} |リンク={{Egov law}} |ウィキソース=温泉法 }}
'''Onsen Law''' (温泉法, 昭和23rd year 法律第125号) is a law in Japan concerning the protection of {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}}.
== Purpose == This law aims to protect hot springs, prevent disasters caused by the extraction of combustible {{ill|Natural gas|en|Natural gas|WD=Q40858|lt=natural gas|1=Natural gas}}, and ensure the proper utilization of hot springs, thereby contributing to the enhancement of {{ill|General welfare clause|en|General welfare clause|WD=Q17157085|lt=public welfare|1=General welfare clause}} (Article 1).
== Content == The law stipulates regulations for land excavation to extract hot springs, permissions for hot spring extraction, appropriate utilization, and display of {{ill|hot spring analysis report|ja|温泉分析書|1=Hot spring analysis report|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hot spring analysis report|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot spring analysis report|qq=}} details.
Recent amendments address the {{ill|Onsen fraud issue|ja|温泉偽装問題|1=Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=Onsen Fraud Scandal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen fraud issue|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=}} by mandating hot spring operators to submit an updated hot spring analysis report every 10 years as prescribed by the Onsen Law Enforcement Order. Following the {{ill|Shibuya Onsen Facility Explosion Incident|ja|渋谷温泉施設爆発事故|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Onsen Facility Explosion Incident|1=Q11561755|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561755}}, stricter guidelines were introduced for land excavation permissions and hot spring extraction permits to prevent disasters caused by combustible natural gas.
== Definition of Onsen == According to Article 2, Paragraph 1, onsen refers to hot water, mineral water, or gases (excluding natural gases primarily composed of hydrocarbons) that meet one of the following criteria:
# Water temperature at the source is 25°C or higher. (Water below 25°C may be referred to as {{ill|Reizei|en|Reizei|WD=Q3423952|lt=cold springs|1=Reizei}} or {{ill|Mineral spring|en|Mineral spring|WD=Q1365924|lt=Mineral spring|1=Mineral spring}}.) # Contains at least one of the following substances in the specified minimum quantities per kilogram: {|class=wikitable style="font-size:small" ! Substance !! Minimum content per kg |- | Dissolved solids (excluding gases) || style="text-align:right"| 1,000 mg |- | Free {{ill|Carbonic acid|en|Carbonic acid|WD=Q104334|lt=carbon dioxide|1=Carbonic acid}} (CO<sub>2</sub>) || style="text-align:right"| 250 mg |- | {{ill|Lithium|en|Lithium|WD=Q568|lt=Lithium ion|1=Lithium}} (Li<sup>+</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 1 mg |- | {{ill|Strontium|en|Strontium|WD=Q938|lt=Strontium ion|1=Strontium}} (Sr<sup>++</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 10 mg |- | {{ill|Barium|en|Barium|WD=Q1112|lt=Barium ion|1=Barium}} (Ba<sup>++</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 5 mg |- | {{ill|Iron|en|Iron|WD=Q677|lt=Iron ions|1=Iron}} (Fe<sup>++</sup>, Fe<sup>+++</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 10 mg |- | Manganese ion (Mn<sup>++</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 10 mg |- | {{ill|Hydrogen|en|Hydrogen|WD=Q556|lt=Hydrogen ion|1=Hydrogen}} (H<sup>+</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 1 mg |- | {{ill|Bromine|en|Bromine|WD=Q879|lt=Bromine ion|1=Bromine}} (Br<sup>-</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 5 mg |- | {{ill|Iodine ion|ja|沃素|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iodine ion|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヨウ素|1=Iodine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1103}} (I<sup>-</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 1 mg |- | {{ill|Fluorine|en|Fluorine|WD=Q650|lt=Fluoride ion|1=Fluorine}} (F<sup>-</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 2 mg |- | Hydrogen arsenate ion (HAsO<sub>4</sub><sup>--</sup>) || style="text-align:right"| 1.3 mg |- | Metarsenous acid (HAsO<sub>2</sub>) || style="text-align:right"| 1 mg |- | Total {{ill|Sulfur|en|Sulfur|WD=Q682|lt=sulfur|1=Sulfur}} (S) [HS<sup>-</sup>, S<sub>2</sub>O<sub>3</sub><sup>--</sup>, H<sub>2</sub>S] || style="text-align:right"| 1 mg |- | {{ill|Boric acid|en|Boric acid|WD=Q187045|lt=Metaboric acid|1=Boric acid}} (HBO<sub>2</sub>) || style="text-align:right"| 5 mg |- | {{ill|Silicic acid|en|Silicic acid|WD=Q12457481|lt=Metasilicic acid|1=Silicic acid}} (H<sub>2</sub>SiO<sub>3</sub>) || style="text-align:right"| 50 mg |- | Sodium bicarbonate (NaHCO<sub>3</sub>) || style="text-align:right"| 340 mg |- | {{ill|Radon|en|Radon|WD=Q1133|lt=Radon|1=Radon}} (Rn) || style="text-align:right"| 20×10<sup>-10</sup> Ci |- | {{ill|Radium|en|Radium|WD=Q1128|lt=Radium salts|1=Radium}} (as Ra) || style="text-align:right"| 1×10<sup>-8</sup> mg |}
== Structure == * Chapter 1: General Provisions (Articles 1-2) * Chapter 2: Protection of Onsen (Articles 3-14) * Chapter 3: Prevention of Disasters Related to Onsen Extraction (Articles 14-2 to 14-10) * Chapter 4: Utilization of Onsen (Articles 15-31) * Chapter 5: Advisory and Hearings (Articles 32-33) * Chapter 6: Miscellaneous Provisions (Articles 34-37) * Chapter 7: Penal Provisions (Articles 38-43) * Supplementary Provisions == See Also == * {{ill|Hot spring analysis report|ja|温泉分析書|1=Hot spring analysis report|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot spring analysis report|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot spring analysis report|qq=}} * {{ill|Mineral Spring Analysis Guidelines|ja|鉱泉分析法指針|1=Guidelines for Mineral Spring Analysis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mineral Spring Analysis Guidelines|13=User:Immanuelle/Guidelines for Mineral Spring Analysis|qq=}} * {{ill|Onsen fraud issue|ja|温泉偽装問題|1=Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=Onsen Fraud Scandal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen fraud issue|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=}} * {{ill|Bathing tax|ja|入湯税|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bathing tax|1=Bath tax|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388992}}
